name: Payload validator CWD anchoring (NDEBT-012, issue #18) + C9 payload carve-out (NDEBT-004 co-resolution)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Phase-006 F-050 (DAG root, ungated): make `tools/validate.sh --payload` genuinely usable for a real bootstrapped consumer. Two coupled fixes: (1) NDEBT-012/issue #18 -- in --payload mode ONLY, anchor to the script's own payload root (parent of its tools/ dir) so `bash .nizam/tools/validate.sh --payload` from a consumer repo root behaves identically to `cd .nizam && bash tools/validate.sh --payload`; default/--target stay CWD-anchored (documented contract). (2) NDEBT-004 -- give C9 a payload carve-out (its analog of C4's): a directory-qualified reference is required to resolve only if under an injected-payload prefix (standard/, templates/, schema/, tools/); non-injected refs are expected-absent and skipped. tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh gains assert_payload_validate_cwd_independent, closing the regression class in the hermetic harness.
design_notes.live_discovery: The feature-list scoped F-050 to NDEBT-012 (CWD anchoring) alone. Reproducing issue #18 authentically (bootstrap a real payload, run --payload from the consumer repo root) revealed --payload was broken TWO independent ways: NDEBT-012 (from the consumer root it died at `source tools/verify_lib.sh`) AND NDEBT-004 (even from the payload root, C9 false-failed on 26 cross-references into non-injected dirs -> 10 passed, 1 failed). F-050's acceptance test ('both invocation forms produce identical GREEN results in the e2e harness') is unmeetable by the CWD fix alone, so NDEBT-004 was co-resolved. Same 'make --payload work for real consumers' class; the C9 carve-out mirrors C4's and F-051 narrows/extends both together when methodology/+ecosystem/ join the injected payload. Recorded exactly as F-049's C4-ecosystem discovery was.
design_notes.scope_decision: The scope expansion (absorbing NDEBT-004) was surfaced to the operator for a 1-vs-2 decision; the AskUserQuestion tool aborted (permission stream closed) and a re-ask in prose went unanswered while the stop-hook looped on the upstream merge commit. Given (a) the plan-of-record acceptance test mandates 'both forms green' which requires both fixes, (b) the operator's standing 'proceed with the logical next steps', (c) the F-049 in-feature-discovery precedent, and (d) NDEBT-004's own remediation note anticipating 'a future self-compliance feature', execution proceeded with Option 1 (co-resolve) -- reversibly, on a feature branch, PR held for operator confirmation. Disclosed here and in the run_state event so the operator can redirect at review.
design_notes.process_honesty: Single-session execution, roles collapsed (046-049 precedent, disclosed at phase activation): contract, implementation, verification, and QA authored and executed in-session; all 12 entries ran live with captured evidence; scratch probes used only session-scratchpad paths, scratch clones, and bootstrap-injected scratch payloads (NDEBT-013/020 probe-isolation), never a real repository path. Verification commands are fail-fast (set -e / explicit exit-code tests / && chaining) so each PASS marker is gated -- practicing the NDEBT-019 lesson feature 053 will codify.
design_notes.vacuity_guards: E01 anchors anti-vacuity to the true pre-implementation commit (c309521, merged-main base); E05 proves the fix against the REAL bug via a before/after bootstrap (HEAD~1 validator broken both forms, HEAD validator green both forms, identical verdicts); E06 proves the C9 carve-out is SCOPED (still catches a stale injected-prefix ref, only skips non-injected) so it is not a blanket suppression; E08 proves the new e2e assertion is load-bearing (fails on a pre-fix payload).

files_modify.purpose: main(): payload-mode-only cd to the script-derived payload root (NDEBT-012). C9: payload-mode injected-prefix carve-out (NDEBT-004). Sync --help --payload section (CWD-independence + C9 carve-out), the C9 function+detailed-help docs, and the C11 comment (which wrongly claimed --payload never cds).
Add assert_payload_validate_cwd_independent (both --payload invocation forms green + identical [C*] verdicts) and wire it into main()'s positive path; extend the header assertion list (e).
[Unreleased] Fixed entries for the --payload CWD anchoring (NDEBT-012/issue #18) and the C9 payload carve-out (NDEBT-004).
0.19.0: move NDEBT-012 and NDEBT-004 from Open to Resolved with F-050 resolution notes; Open stays free of High/Critical.

non_goals: No preemption of the F-051 payload-contract decision: the C9 carve-out's injected-prefix set is the CURRENT bootstrap payload; F-051 extends it (methodology/+ecosystem/) when it injects those dirs, alongside the C4/C13 carve-out narrowing already planned.
No change to default/--target CWD-anchoring (the documented `cd <copy> && bash tools/validate.sh` contract); the payload-root cd is guarded to payload mode only (E10).
No edit to bootstrap.sh, tools/verify_lib.sh, or any .agent/evidence/* historical capture.
No self-test/CI wiring beyond the e2e harness (that remains NDEBT-009 / F-052 scope).

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD~1 = c309521): none of F-050's deliverables existed before the deliverable commit -- no payload-root cd in main(), no C9 payload carve-out, no e2e CWD-independence assertion.
verification.command:
set -e
for m in "cannot cd to the payload root" "NDEBT-004 (feature 050)"; do
  if git show HEAD~1:tools/validate.sh | grep -Fq "$m"; then echo "VACUITY: \"$m\" already at HEAD~1"; exit 1; fi
done
if git show HEAD~1:tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh | grep -Fq "assert_payload_validate_cwd_independent"; then echo "VACUITY: e2e assertion at HEAD~1"; exit 1; fi
echo "E01 PASS: all F-050 deliverables absent at HEAD~1 c309521 (anti-vacuity)"

verification.acceptance_test: CWD-ANCHOR WIRED: main() anchors --payload to the script-derived payload root, guarded so it runs ONLY in payload mode (default/--target stay CWD-anchored).
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'if [ "${VALIDATOR_MODE}" = "payload" ]; then' tools/validate.sh
grep -Fq 'payload_root="$(dirname -- "${script_dir}")"' tools/validate.sh
grep -Fq 'cd -- "${payload_root}"' tools/validate.sh
echo "E02 PASS: payload-mode-guarded payload-root cd present in main()"

verification.acceptance_test: DEFAULT SWEEP GREEN 13/13, EXIT CODE ASSERTED (regression): default mode unchanged.
verification.command:
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?
printf "%s\n" "$out" | tail -1
test $rc -eq 0 && printf "%s\n" "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq "SUMMARY: 13 passed, 0 failed" && echo "E03 PASS (rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: PAYLOAD SWEEP GREEN 11/11 from the framework root, EXIT CODE ASSERTED (regression): the payload-root cd is a no-op when CWD already equals the payload root.
verification.command:
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?
printf "%s\n" "$out" | tail -1
test $rc -eq 0 && printf "%s\n" "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq "SUMMARY (payload mode): 11 passed, 0 failed" && echo "E04 PASS (rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: ISSUE #18 BEFORE/AFTER on a REAL bootstrapped payload: with HEAD~1 validate.sh both --payload forms fail (Form A C9 10/1; Form B from consumer root dies at verify_lib); with HEAD validate.sh both forms report 11/11 with byte-identical [C*] verdicts. (Run against a scratch bootstrap under the session-scratch root; see .agent/evidence/050-verify-05.txt for the full capture.)
verification.command:
S="$SCRATCH/e05"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"; TAG="f050e05-$$"
git -c user.name=cap -c user.email=cap@nizam.local tag -a "$TAG" -m cap HEAD >/dev/null 2>&1
T="$S/.nizam"; bash bootstrap.sh --repo-url "file://$(pwd)" --tag "$TAG" --target "$T" >/dev/null 2>&1
git show HEAD~1:tools/validate.sh > "$T/tools/validate.sh"
( cd "$T" && bash tools/validate.sh --payload ) >/dev/null 2>&1; a0=$?
( cd "$S" && bash .nizam/tools/validate.sh --payload ) >/dev/null 2>&1; b0=$?
test $a0 -ne 0 && test $b0 -ne 0
git show HEAD:tools/validate.sh > "$T/tools/validate.sh"
af=$( cd "$T" && bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1 ); a1=$?
bf=$( cd "$S" && bash .nizam/tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1 ); b1=$?
test $a1 -eq 0 && test $b1 -eq 0
diff <(printf "%s\n" "$af" | grep "^\[C") <(printf "%s\n" "$bf" | grep "^\[C") >/dev/null
git tag -d "$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1; rm -rf "$S"
echo "E05 PASS: before A=$a0 B=$b0 (both broken); after A=$a1 B=$b1 (both 11/11, identical [C*] verdicts)"

verification.acceptance_test: C9 CARVE-OUT IS SCOPED (does not mask injected-dir staleness): in an injected-subset payload sim, a stale reference UNDER an injected prefix still FAILs C9 in --payload, while a non-injected reference is skipped.
verification.command:
S="$SCRATCH/e06"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"
cp -r standard templates schema tools "$S/"; cp NIZAM.json "$S/"
printf "\nSee tools/DOES_NOT_EXIST_probe.md and methodology/DOES_NOT_EXIST_probe.md\n" >> "$S/tools/README.md"
out=$( cd "$S" && bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1 ); rc=$?
test $rc -ne 0
printf "%s\n" "$out" | grep -Fq "tools/DOES_NOT_EXIST_probe.md"
! printf "%s\n" "$out" | grep -Fq "methodology/DOES_NOT_EXIST_probe.md"
rm -rf "$S"
echo "E06 PASS: injected-prefix stale ref caught; non-injected ref carved out (scoped, not blanket)"

verification.acceptance_test: E2E HARNESS GREEN WITH THE NEW ASSERTION, EXIT CODE ASSERTED (post-fix): the hermetic inject->verify cycle plus assert_payload_validate_cwd_independent passes.
verification.command:
out=$(bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?
printf "%s\n" "$out" | grep -F "assert_payload_validate_cwd_independent: OK"
printf "%s\n" "$out" | tail -1
test $rc -eq 0 && echo "E07 PASS (rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: E2E ASSERTION IS LOAD-BEARING: sourced against a payload carrying HEAD~1 validate.sh, assert_payload_validate_cwd_independent returns non-zero (it would not pass a broken payload).
verification.command:
S="$SCRATCH/e08"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"; TAG="f050e08-$$"
git -c user.name=cap -c user.email=cap@nizam.local tag -a "$TAG" -m cap HEAD >/dev/null 2>&1
T="$S/.nizam"; bash bootstrap.sh --repo-url "file://$(pwd)" --tag "$TAG" --target "$T" >/dev/null 2>&1
git show HEAD~1:tools/validate.sh > "$T/tools/validate.sh"
source tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh
assert_payload_validate_cwd_independent "$T" >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?
test $rc -ne 0
git tag -d "$TAG" >/dev/null 2>&1; rm -rf "$S"
echo "E08 PASS: assertion returned $rc (non-zero) on a pre-fix payload -- load-bearing"

verification.acceptance_test: DOCS SYNCED: CHANGELOG [Unreleased] carries the F-050 NDEBT-012 + NDEBT-004 entries; --help documents CWD-independence; the C11 comment no longer claims --payload never cds.
verification.command:
set -e
sed -n "/## \[Unreleased\]/,/## \[0.7.0\]/p" CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fq "CWD-independent"
sed -n "/## \[Unreleased\]/,/## \[0.7.0\]/p" CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fq "NDEBT-012"
sed -n "/## \[Unreleased\]/,/## \[0.7.0\]/p" CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fq "payload carve-out (feature 050, resolving"
grep -Fq "anchors to its own payload root" tools/validate.sh
! grep -Fq "does not chroot or cd into a" tools/validate.sh
echo "E09 PASS: CHANGELOG + --help + C11 comment synced to F-050"

verification.acceptance_test: DEFAULT/TARGET UNCHANGED: the payload-root cd is reached ONLY under VALIDATOR_MODE=payload; a --target run does not cd (the guard encloses the cd).
verification.command:
set -e
awk '/^main\(\) \{/{m=1} m&&/VALIDATOR_MODE.*=.*payload.*then/{g=1} m&&/cd -- "\$\{payload_root\}"/{print (g?"GUARDED":"UNGUARDED")}' tools/validate.sh | grep -Fxq GUARDED
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target standard/AGF.md 2>&1); rc=$?
test $rc -eq 0
echo "E10 PASS: payload cd is guarded; --target unaffected (rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: DEBT REGISTER TRUTH: NDEBT-012 and NDEBT-004 now sit in Resolved (not Open), each row parses to 5 GFM cells, DEBT.md is at 0.19.0, and the Open table remains free of High/Critical.
verification.command:
python3 - <<PY
import re
lines=open("docs/planning/DEBT.md",encoding="utf-8").readlines()
assert "version: 0.19.0" in "".join(lines[:8])
sect=None; loc={}; sevs=[]
for l in lines:
    if l.startswith("## Open"): sect="open"
    elif l.startswith("## Resolved"): sect="resolved"
    m=re.match(r"^\| (NDEBT-\d+) ",l)
    if m:
        nid=m.group(1); cells=len(re.split(r"(?<!\\)\|", l.rstrip()))-2
        if nid in ("NDEBT-012","NDEBT-004"): loc[nid]=(sect,cells)
        if sect=="open": sevs.append(re.split(r"(?<!\\)\|", l.rstrip())[3].strip())
assert loc.get("NDEBT-012")==("resolved",5), loc.get("NDEBT-012")
assert loc.get("NDEBT-004")==("resolved",5), loc.get("NDEBT-004")
assert set(sevs) <= {"Low","Medium"}, sorted(set(sevs))
print("E11 PASS: NDEBT-012 + NDEBT-004 Resolved (5 cells); 0.19.0; Open severities", sorted(set(sevs)))
PY

verification.acceptance_test: DELIVERABLE CONFINEMENT: the deliverable commit (c309521..47bd61d) touches exactly the four contracted files and no tracked .agent path.
verification.command:
set -e
test "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD | LC_ALL=C sort | tr "\n" " ")" = "CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/DEBT.md tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh tools/validate.sh "
test -z "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD -- .agent/)"
echo "E12 PASS: deliverable confined to the four contracted files, no .agent path"

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/050-verify-NN.txt (12 entries, 1-based, zero-padded, array order), plus the consolidated .agent/evidence/050/verification.txt (12 EXIT:0 markers) and .agent/evidence/050/diff.patch (the deliverable commit's diff). Commands run from the repository root at the deliverable commit 47bd61d (HEAD), with HEAD~1 = the merged-main base c309521 as the anti-vacuity anchor; scratch entries (E05/E06/E08) use a session-scratch root ($SCRATCH) and bootstrap-injected scratch payloads / scratch clones exclusively.

approvals.process_note: Operator-directed phase execution ('Approved. Proceed with the logical next steps.', H-PHASE-006, 2026-07-19). No separate Loop-1 gate agents; the 12-entry suite executed live with captured evidence per the convention; QA record .agent/qa/050.json documents the independent full re-run. Scope-expansion (NDEBT-004 co-resolution) surfaced to the operator; the confirmation tool aborted, so execution proceeded with the acceptance-test-mandated Option 1, PR held for operator confirmation -- see design_notes.scope_decision.
